task_name: EUREP Educational Overview - Multiple FLS/BASIC Urology Tasks
cannot_score_reason: This is an instructional/educational video compilation, not a trainee performance video
video_content_summary:
This is a promotional and educational video from EUREP (European hands-on training) showcasing their BASIC Laparoscopic Urological Skills program. The video includes:
- EUREP organization branding and facility overview (24 stations)
- Instructor/presenter segments in professional attire
- Demonstration footage of Peg Transfer task (visible at ~2:29 mark showing trainer setup and suture/knot tying station)
- Endoscopic stone treatment demonstration
- Educational graphics and titles
- No actual trainee performance being scored

frames_analyzed.content: EUREP hands-on training logo with instructor portrait
Conference/classroom setting with 'over 300 EBLUS exams' text
'European BASIC Laparoscopic Urological skills' title slide
Peg Transfer trainer setup - dual camera view showing pegs, colored objects, instruments - LAP CAMERA and UPPER CAMERA labeled at 00:06:24
Knot Tying station demonstration - dual camera view showing gauze/drain material, instruments at 02:29:24 timestamp
Endoscopic stone treatment - high magnification view of tissue with laser treatment (surgical/clinical content, not FLS trainer)
Endoscopic view of tissue/organ - clinical demonstration

why_not_scored:
This video is clearly educational/promotional material created by EUREP hands-on training to showcase their program. Key indicators:
1. Multiple cut scenes with different topics and speakers
2. Title cards and organizational branding throughout
3. Presenter segments with professional setup and lighting changes
4. No continuous task performance video
5. Includes clinical footage (endoscopic stone treatment) which is not an FLS task
6. While brief glimpses of Peg Transfer and knot tying trainers are visible, these are not complete, uninterrupted performance recordings suitable for scoring
7. No clear task start/stop or performance timing evident
recommendation:
This video should not be scored. If training evaluation is needed, request:
- Complete, uninterrupted single-task performance video
- Clear task start and completion points
- Consistent camera angle throughout
- Trainee performing without narration or interruption
